description: Fix the DELETE /api/admin/eids/:eid endpoint to fully clean up all agent data when an EID is removed, including the agent record, number references, socket connections, and real-time broadcast.
steps: In server.js, locate the DELETE /api/admin/eids/:eid handler at approximately line 1751. Currently it only does: delete appState.allowedEids[eid]; saveState(appState); res.json({ success: true });
Add cleanup logic BEFORE saveState to: (a) Compute the agentId as 'emp_' + eid. (b) Delete appState.agents[agentId] to remove the agent runtime record entirely.

Release numbers currently assigned to that agent: iterate appState.numbers and for any number where n.assignedTo === agentId, set n.assignedTo = null (releases the number back to the pool for another agent to pick up).

Handle followups locked by that agent: iterate appState.numbers and for any number where n.followupLockedBy === agentId, set n.followupLockedBy = null (releases the followup lock so other agents or admin can manage it). Do NOT change the disposition - it stays as 'followup' so the lead is not lost.

Handle interested leads owned by that agent: iterate appState.numbers and for any number where n.interestedBy === agentId, set n.interestedBy = null (clears ownership so admin can see and reassign). Do NOT remove the lead or change its disposition - it stays as 'interested'.

Force disconnect any active socket for the removed agent: use io.emit('force-stop-agent', { agentId }) to notify all clients. This follows the existing pattern of io.emit('force-stop') used in the reset endpoints at lines 1286 and 1315.

After all cleanup is done and saveState(appState) is called, call broadcastAdminStats() to push updated stats to admin panel. This ensures the admin immediately sees the agent disappear from the live stats.

Optionally (low risk): Do NOT remove entries from appState.dialedLog where entry.agentId === agentId. The dialed log is historical data and should be preserved for reporting purposes. The log entries already store agentName so they remain useful even after the agent record is deleted.

Verify: The /api/admin/agents-list endpoint (line 1117) already iterates both appState.agents and appState.allowedEids. Since we delete from both, the removed agent will no longer appear in this list - no change needed here.

Verify: The /api/rankings endpoint (line 1860) initializes agentScores from appState.agents and appState.allowedEids. Since both are cleaned, the removed agent will not appear in future rankings. Historical dialedLog entries from the removed agent will still create an entry in agentScores (via the dialedLog loop) but that is acceptable for historical accuracy - OR if desired, also filter out removed agents from rankings by checking if the agentId still exists in appState.agents or appState.allowedEids.
acceptance_criteria: After DELETE /api/admin/eids/:eid is called, appState.agents['emp_<eid>'] no longer exists
After deletion, no number in appState.numbers has assignedTo set to the removed agent's ID
After deletion, no number in appState.numbers has followupLockedBy set to the removed agent's ID
After deletion, numbers that had interestedBy set to the removed agent now have interestedBy = null but retain disposition = 'interested'
After deletion, broadcastAdminStats() is called so admin panel updates in real-time
After deletion, a socket event is emitted to force-disconnect the removed agent
The /api/admin/agents-list endpoint no longer returns the removed agent
The /api/rankings endpoint no longer includes the removed agent in current rankings (historical log entries are acceptable)
verification: Run: cd /projects/sandbox/DSA-CRM- && npm install && timeout 5 node server.js || true (confirm server starts without crash)
Manually inspect the DELETE endpoint in server.js to confirm all cleanup steps are present
findings: Syntax check passes (node --check server.js). npm install cannot complete due to network restrictions (403 from squid proxy), but node_modules partially exists and no new dependencies were added. Server startup test cannot run without express installed but the code change is purely additive logic in an existing endpoint and verified syntactically correct.
